title: Podman Asciinemas Archived
body:
**Description**
Almost all the asciinemas in https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/commands.md are archived and unavailable. The demos for our commands are no longer viewable as such.

comments.body:
@mheon As a POC, I pulled the images asciinema, did some hacking and dropped it on the podman.io site at:
https://podman.io/asciinema/

The page is a bit rough atm as I didn't create a title or what not, but we could drop them all onto podman.io if you'd like and run them from there.

If this looks good, I'll change that link to an index.html file that links to each command we've an asciinema for.
When an asciinema is made, we'd have to save it as a json file, make a html page for it and add a link to it on the index.html in podman.io  and then also on commands.md here.

The html page for each is pretty straight forward, mostly a cut/paste job.  Here's the current one for images for your info:

```
<html>
<head>
  <link rel="stylesheet" type="text/css" href="/asciinema/asciinema-player.css" />
</head>
<body>
  <asciinema-player src="/asciinema/images.json" cols="211" rows="52"></asciinema-player>
  <script src="/asciinema/asciinema-player.js"></script>
</body>
</html>
```
Well it is also time that we re-did a bunch of them, since a lot of them are kpod commands.
Do we need to keep this issue open?  Good summer intern project would be to rereate all of the ascicinema on the commands, force intern to learn commands.
@TomSweeneyRedHat did you have luck in restoring these?

I'd keep it open.  I restored one as  POC, but most are gone.  When I get some time I need to polish off the framework, save the few remaining ones and document the process.  We can then fill them in as we have time and/or set an intern on them.
I think it would be awesome to have all the asciinemas also as demos in containers/Demos. @TomSweeneyRedHat, let me know if I can assist you there. This sounds like a lot of work.
Just noticed that I left some space for interpretation. What I mean is that all the demos we show in the asciinemas would be nice to have as shell scripts in containers/Demos as well.

That's a good throught vrothberg, probably a bit more work, but probably worthwhile in the end.   However that would mean killing all the videos we currently have as there weren't any scripts used that I know of for them.  Thinking a bit more, if someone updated a demo script later on down the line, we'd have to make sure the video got updated too.

I've got the `podman images` command all setup and changed the commands.md to accept the new column for the script.  I need to add a README.md somewhere showing the steps necessary to make similar demos and casts for the other commands.  I've a rough cut of that document in a draft email, need to finish it up and drop it somewhere.  Most likely containers/Demos/podman_cli with a link from commands.md to it.

If @mrunalp is hip, we might want to think about adding a jira card for each command and then close this out, then we can assign them as needed.  Or just leave this up until we knock them all out or make a bunch of issues in here.
@baude I've 3 commands of 50+ covered.  Given the interns are soon departing for the year, should we keep this open until all are done, move to jira, just close this now, or something else?
Move it to Jira and close this.
